goal: Add RLS policies to all migration schema tables — CLAUDE.md compliance

acceptance_criteria.description: RLS enabled on all migration.* content tables: engagement, quality_profile, field_mapping, code_mapping, batch, lineage, exception, reconciliation, correction, analyst_decision, gate_transition, notification, risk, event, certification_record. Policy pattern matches CLAUDE.md Section 6: tenant scoping via engagement_members join.

Migration SQL file creates policies using the standard pattern: USING (engagement_id IN (SELECT engagement_id FROM migration.engagement WHERE tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID)). Tables without direct engagement_id use a join path (e.g., batch via engagement_id, reconciliation via batch.engagement_id).
All existing migration API tests still pass with RLS enabled — no test relies on cross-tenant data access
Test verifies that a query with tenant A's context cannot read tenant B's engagement data

critical: Every migration content table has an RLS policy — no exceptions
RLS policies use current_setting('app.current_tenant_id'), matching the platform's auth middleware pattern
Tables without direct tenant_id column use a join path that is covered by an index
Force row-level security enabled (ALTER TABLE ... FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY)
high: Migration file numbered correctly after the latest existing migration
Existing tests updated to set app.current_tenant_id in test DB context
No performance regression — RLS join paths use indexed columns
medium: Document the RLS policy pattern in a code comment for future migration table additions
